title: Sequential Extraction and Immunoblotting
author: Isabel Lam
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bu2wnyfe
materials: Dilute the primary antibodies in TBS/5% (wt/vol) milk and incubate them Overnight at 4 °C with shaking.

steps.title: Rinse the neurons twice with PBS
steps.instruction: Rinse the neurons twice with PBS.

steps.title: Place the dish On ice
steps.instruction: Place the dish On ice . By working one well at a time, completely aspirate the PBS and add the following volumes of ice-cold 1% (vol/vol) TX-100/TBS with protease and phosphatase inhibitors: 250 µL per well for a six-well plate, and 500 µL for a 6-cm dish.

steps.title: Use a cell scraper to thoroughly...
steps.instruction: Use a cell scraper to thoroughly scrape all neurons from each well.

steps.title: Place the neurons in a polyallomar...
steps.instruction: Place the neurons in a polyallomar tube for a table top ultracentrifuge; keep the tube On ice .

steps.title: Sonicate the tube ten times at a 0
steps.instruction: Sonicate the tube ten times at a 0.5-s pulse and at 10% power.

steps.title: Incubate the tube On ice for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube On ice for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 100000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 100000 x g, °C, at 4 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 4× Laemmli buffer to ~...
steps.instruction: Add 4× Laemmli buffer to ~ 150 µL – 200 µL of TX-100 supernatant . Save ~ 20 µL of supernatant for protein assay. Retain the supernatant On ice or in a -20 °C freezer. Some of the supernatant is usually lost during Step 3–8; we recommend measuring the remaining supernatant before determining how much to remove for Laemmli buffer.

steps.title: To the pellet, add the same...
steps.instruction: To the pellet, add the same volume of ice-cold 1% (vol/vol) TX-100/TBS with protease and phosphatase inhibitors: 250 µL per well for a six-well plate, and 500 µL for a 6 cm dish.

steps.title: Sonicate ten times at a 0
steps.instruction: Sonicate ten times at a 0.5-s pulse and at 10% power. Keep the tip of the probe toward the bottom of the tube to prevent frothing. Make sure that the pellet is completely dispersed. a. Sonicator: Fisherbrand™ Model 120 Sonic Dismembrator (120V, 50/60Hz) b. I used Misonix Sonicator S-4000 with Program Settings: Amplitude 10, Process Time 00:00:10 , Pulse-ON time: 00:00:01 , Pulse-OFF time: 00:00:01

steps.title: Centrifuge the mixture at 100000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the mixture at 100000 x g, °C, at 4 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 2% (vol/vol) SDS/TBS to the...
steps.instruction: Add 2% (vol/vol) SDS/TBS to the pellet with protease and phosphatases inhibitors. To a six-well plate, add 125 µL of 2% (wt/vol) SDS/TBS per well, and to a 6 cm dish add 250 µL of 2% (wt/vol) SDS/TBS.

steps.title: Sonicate 15 times, at a 0
steps.instruction: Sonicate 15 times, at a 0.5-s pulse and at 10% power. Keep the tip of the probe toward the bottom of the tube. Make sure that the pellet is completely dispersed. a. I used Misonix Sonicator S-4000 with Program Settings: Amplitude 10, Process Time 00:00:15, Pulse-ON time: 00:00:01, Pulse-OFF time: 00:00:01

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and place it...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and place it into a new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Perform a BCA/protein assay on TX-100 supernatant and SDS extract
steps.instruction: Perform a BCA/protein assay on TX-100 supernatant and SDS extract. Typically, a 1:5 dilution for the BCA assay is sufficient.

steps.title: Dilute 2% (wt/vol) SDS extract from...
steps.instruction: Dilute 2% (wt/vol) SDS extract from Step 15 into Laemmli buffer to 2× volume for the corresponding TX-100 fraction (regardless of the protein concentration of the SDS fraction). For example, if you have 180 µL of TX-100 extract (from Step 8) at 1 mg/mL and 90 µL of SDS extract, add 60 µL of 4× Laemmli buffer to the TX-100 extract and 30 µL of 4× Laemmli buffer to the SDS extract. Load 12.5 µL of both the TX-100 extract ( 10 g ) and SDS extract. We suggest using a 2× volume because it makes the insoluble a-syn species more abundant and thus easier to visualize and quantify by immunoblotting.

steps.title: Load the samples on a 4–20%...
steps.instruction: Load the samples on a 4–20% (wt/vol) gel and run them according to the manufacturer’s directions. We use 85 V constant voltage until the dye front runs off the gel. Be sure not to let the 10-kDa marker to run off the gel.

steps.title: Transfer the proteins from the gel...
steps.instruction: Transfer the proteins from the gel to a nitrocellulose membrane according to the manufacturer’s instructions at 100 V for 01:15:00 or Overnight at 40 V.

steps.title: Block the membrane for 01:00:00 with TBS/5% (wt/vol) milk
steps.instruction: Block the membrane for 01:00:00 with TBS/5% (wt/vol) milk.

steps.title: Rinse the membrane three times with TBS/T, 00:10:00 each rinse
steps.instruction: Rinse the membrane three times with TBS/T, 00:10:00 each rinse.

steps.title: Incubate the membrane with HRP-conjugated secondary...
steps.instruction: Incubate the membrane with HRP-conjugated secondary antibodies for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Rinse the membrane three times with TBS/T, 00:10:00 each rinse
steps.instruction: Rinse the membrane three times with TBS/T, 00:10:00 each rinse.

steps.title: Use Misonix Sonicator S-4000, with Program Settings
steps.instruction: Use Misonix Sonicator S-4000, with Program Settings: Amplitude 10, Process Time 00:00:10 , Pulse-ON time 00:00:01 , Pulse-OFF time 00:00:01 .

steps.title: Wipe sonication tip with 1% SDS...
steps.instruction: Wipe sonication tip with 1% SDS after coming into contact with PFFs, then 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Optima MAX-TL Ultracentrifuge TLA-120
steps.instruction: Optima MAX-TL Ultracentrifuge TLA-120.2 S/N 12U1811 100000 x g, °C, 00:30:00 4 °C .
